board_csa_architect.notes: Solid architecture. tier_policies table with org-level overrides follows the established pattern. UNIQUE(org_id, feature_key) prevents duplicate policies. Migration path is incremental.

board_ciso_cybersecurity.notes: Default-deny is correct. RLS on tier_policies prevents cross-tenant policy tampering. System policies (org_id=NULL) immutable to non-Org-0. Permissions manifest only exposes applicable policies.

board_caio_fullstack.notes: AI tier awareness is a clean addition. Chat agent checks tier before surfacing sensitive widgets. No VRAM impact — just a metadata check at runtime.

board_vp_ops_devops.notes: Zero-downtime migration. Seed tier_policies with current hardcoded values first, then swap components. If tier_policies table is empty, privilegeGate falls back to hardcoded — safe degradation.

board_red_team.notes: APPROVE WITH CONDITIONS: (1) Policy escalation attack: if a tenant admin can create org overrides that LOWER tier requirements, they could grant themselves access to exec-only features. MUST validate that org overrides can only RAISE the bar, never lower it below system default. (2) Cache poisoning: permissions manifest must be server-authoritative — never trust client-side cached policy for write operations. (3) Anti-AC: MUST NEVER allow an org override to set a min_tier LOWER than the system default for the same feature_key.
